title: erigeron neglectus
intro: Erigeron neglectus, also known as the variegated fleabane or daisy fleabane, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright white and pink flowers with daisy-like appearances. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Erigeron neglectus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its dried flowers can be made into a poultice or infused in oil for topical applications.
